audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's lament over human service, sleepless nights, fleeting life, divine surveillance, Sea/Monster imagery, and the plea to be left alone before death. Sensitive death-language is restrained while preserving the lament.

verses.restored: Is there not hard service for a mortal on earth, and are not his days like the days of a hired worker?
Like a servant who pants for shade, and like a hired worker who waits for his wage,
so I have been made to inherit months of emptiness, and nights of trouble have been appointed to me.
When I lie down, I say, When shall I rise? But the night stretches on, and I am filled with tossing until dawn.
My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin hardens and breaks open.
My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle, and they come to an end without hope.
Remember that my life is breath; my eye will not return to see good.
The eye of the one seeing me will not behold me; your eyes will be on me, but I will be gone.
A cloud vanishes and goes away; so the one who goes down to Sheol does not come up.
He does not return again to his house, and his place knows him no more.
Therefore I will not restrain my mouth. I will speak in the distress of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Am I the Sea, or the Sea-Monster, that you set a guard over me?
When I say, My bed will comfort me, my couch will lift my complaint,
then you terrify me with dreams and frighten me with visions,
so that my soul chooses an ending; death seems preferable to this body of bones.
I reject it; I will not live forever. Leave me alone, for my days are breath.
What is a human, that you make so much of him, and that you set your heart on him,
that you inspect him every morning and test him every moment?
How long will you not look away from me, nor leave me alone even for a moment?
If I have sinned, what have I done to you, watcher of humanity? Why have you made me your target, so that I have become a burden to myself?
Why do you not lift away my transgression and pass over my guilt? For now I will lie down in the dust; you will seek me, but I will not be.
